title: HFT Latency
description: MD pipeline latency (per-second aggregates from queue replay, hft.md_stats), strategy decision latency (orders), and end-of-run latency report summaries (hft.latency_reports). recv→pub is same-host and skew-free; exch→* segments are signed and include cross-clock skew.

panels.title: EC2 live p50 (imported every ~1min from journald, not a whole-run report)
panels.description: label=ec2-live: periodic interval [latency] lines scraped from the running EC2 processes via SSM (scripts/reporting/import_latency_live.ps1), no restart involved. Separate from the one-off 'ec2' snapshot in the table below.

targets.rawSql: SELECT reportTs AS ts, multiIf(segment = 'exch->recv (epoch, skew-bounded)', '1. Receive market data (exchange network)', segment = 'recv->parse', '2. Parse WS frame', segment = 'parse->apply', '3. Rebuild order book', segment = 'apply->publish (queue write)', '4. Publish to queue', segment = 'recv->apply (total in-process)', 'Total: receive -> book updated', segment = 'recv->publish (total incl. queue)', 'Total: receive -> queue published', segment) AS series, p50Us FROM hft.latency_reports WHERE label='ec2-live' AND $__timeFilter(reportTs) AND n > 0 ORDER BY reportTs

panels.title: EC2 live p99 (imported every ~1min from journald, not a whole-run report)
panels.description: Same feed as the p50 panel above (label=ec2-live, scripts/reporting/import_latency_live.ps1) but plotting p99Us -- the tail behavior, not the typical case.

targets.rawSql: SELECT reportTs AS ts, multiIf(segment = 'exch->recv (epoch, skew-bounded)', '1. Receive market data (exchange network)', segment = 'recv->parse', '2. Parse WS frame', segment = 'parse->apply', '3. Rebuild order book', segment = 'apply->publish (queue write)', '4. Publish to queue', segment = 'recv->apply (total in-process)', 'Total: receive -> book updated', segment = 'recv->publish (total incl. queue)', 'Total: receive -> queue published', segment) AS series, p99Us FROM hft.latency_reports WHERE label='ec2-live' AND $__timeFilter(reportTs) AND n > 0 ORDER BY reportTs

panels.title: End-of-run latency reports (all runs, µs)
panels.description: Parsed from reports*/latency-*.txt. Backtest runs report n=0 by design (replay latency vs wall clock is meaningless).

targets.rawSql: SELECT label AS run, reportTs AS report, segment, n, round(p50Us, 1) AS "p50 µs", round(p99Us, 1) AS "p99 µs", round(p999Us, 1) AS "p99.9 µs", round(maxUs, 1) AS "max µs" FROM hft.latency_reports WHERE n > 0 ORDER BY reportTs DESC, segment
